operational_conditions.condition: Session type enforcement strategy must be defined before W1 receptor implementation begins
Non-interference proof must be validated against all 5 persona families with concrete state examples before W5 Trustee deployment
Write-behind evidence recovery must be demonstrated in W1 integration tests

monitoring_flags.trigger: W1 implementation cannot achieve compile-time session type checking
Evidence loss detected between response emit and epoch boundary commit
Composed receptor chain produces inconsistent results due to shared state mutation
More than 50% of receptors require significant redesign during implementation

rationale: C36 EMA-I fills a critical architectural gap — every external interaction with Atrahasis was assumed but never specified. The four-component architecture (Receptors, STE, IEC, PPE) is formally grounded in session types, Galois connections, causal evidence chains, and non-interference proofs. Scope is correctly controlled: NL deferred to v2.0, receptor contract over receptor catalog, 3 exemplars rather than 35 full definitions. Integration density is highest in the stack (18 inbound + 4 outbound). 4-claim portfolio represents genuine intellectual contribution.
